structural.modification.structural_js.name: structural.js
structural.modification.structural_js.description:
Author:  Sébastien Pierre
License: Revised BSD License

modification.modification.name: modification
modification.modification.description: Provides rich-text formatting operations that mutate the DOM tree managed by an Editor.

modification.Modification.name: Modification
modification.Modification.description:
Rich-text formatting operations and DOM mutations.
- session: 
- editor: 
- schema:

content:
is a lightweight toolkit for building structural, document-backed,
and rich text editors for the Web. It is designed to use the DOM as the source of truth
for the document. Because it doesn't require an AST

, it avoids complex synchronization
or serialization layers, working seamlessly with any web framework or raw DOM-based rendering.

Instead of managing a custom schema and AST representation, Structural operates directly on standard
DOM elements, using class-based semantic markup

to model structured nodes. The cursor can traverse the document linearly using keyboard navigation or point-and-click,
treating read-only or template structures as cohesive, interactive boundaries.
You can learn more about each component:
: DOM-backed coordinate mapping and positions index ―
: Rich text and block structure mutations ―

meta.code:
Editable Document

Modify this text, or interact with this {AtomicToken}.

<script type="importmap">
{
  "imports": {
    "structural/editor": "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/sebastien/structural.js@v0.1.0/src/js/structural/editor.js",
    "structural/modification": "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/sebastien/structural.js@v0.1.0/src/js/structural/modification.js"
  }
}
</script>

<script type="module">
import { Editor } from "structural/editor";
import { Modification } from "structural/modification";
</script>

content: : Main orchestrator wrapping the DOM tree, initializing the text adapter and text inputs.
: Linear mapping agent indexing the DOM tree structure into editable caret positions.
: State-holder driving horizontal/vertical movement, selection, and mutation coordinates.
: Virtual caret placement agent responsible for drawing and positioning the visual caret.
: Selection coordinator mapping native or virtual ranges to structural element boundaries.
: Graphic helper drawing virtual highlighting rectangles aligned with current ranges.
: High-level utility for mutative rich-text styling
, coordinating DOM MutationObservers and translating structural layout to a linear index space.
: Demonstrates a standard rich text editor toolbar with keyboard shortcuts and inline/block mutation commands.
: Illustrates inline annotation structures like atomic variables and editable mention elements
: Features DOM-backed inline document feedback and annotations where suggestions live alongside primary text.
: Showcases a structured email template builder with conditional blocks, repeats, placeholders, and interactive fields.
: No AST or intermediate JSON schemas are required; state is mapped directly to content elements.
: Precise visual selection blocks and caret alignments that integrate perfectly without breaking layout or native select behaviors.
: Multi-directional key navigation keeping track of column boundaries, letter spacing, and line moves
: Safe toggling of rich-text decorations with layout-aware unwrapping, replacement, and block promotion guards.
: Zero dependencies, works natively using modern ES modules
